title: Raycast basics
goal: Cast a ray from a Part and react to what it hits.

assets.name: Emitter rig
assets.description: Pre-built target mesh for the ray to hit.

steps.body:
Raycasting lets you detect what a ray crosses in the world. In this
lesson we'll build an **emitter** part in Workspace that fires a ray
straight down whenever it's touched, then drop in a target for the
ray to hit.
steps.explain:
Raycasting is used everywhere: line-of-sight checks, projectile
weapons, laser pointers, cursor picking, AI vision cones. The
primitive is always the same — an origin point plus a direction
vector — and the world tells you what (if anything) the ray crossed.

steps.body:
First, create the **Emitter** part in Workspace. Flat and anchored so
it stays put and it's obvious where the ray starts from.

Add a **Handler** script under the emitter. On Touched, it casts a
ray 50 studs straight down and prints what it hit (if anything).
steps.explain:
`workspace:Raycast(origin, direction)` returns a `RaycastResult` or
`nil`. The direction's magnitude is the ray's length — `Vector3.yAxis * -50`
means "50 studs downward."
steps.source:
local emitter = script.Parent

local function onTouch(hit)
  local origin = emitter.Position
  local direction = Vector3.yAxis * -50
  local result = workspace:Raycast(origin, direction)
  if result then
    print("Ray hit:", result.Instance:GetFullName(), "at", result.Position)
  else
    print("Ray hit nothing.")
  end
end

emitter.Touched:Connect(onTouch)

steps.body:
The emitter will detect itself if we're not careful. Add a
`RaycastParams` that filters out the emitter so we only see *other*
things the ray crosses.
steps.source:
local emitter = script.Parent

local params = RaycastParams.new()
params.FilterType = Enum.RaycastFilterType.Exclude
params.FilterDescendantsInstances = { emitter }

local function onTouch(hit)
  local origin = emitter.Position
  local direction = Vector3.yAxis * -50
  local result = workspace:Raycast(origin, direction, params)
  if result then
    print("Ray hit:", result.Instance:GetFullName(), "at", result.Position)
  else
    print("Ray hit nothing.")
  end
end

emitter.Touched:Connect(onTouch)

steps.body:
Clone in the bundled target rig so the ray has something interesting
to hit. It lands below the emitter by default.
Nudge the target directly below the emitter so the ray hits it cleanly.

Right now the ray only fires when something touches the emitter.
Let's make it fire continuously instead — a good moment to hand
off to Studio's Assistant.
steps.suggestedPrompt:
Modify Workspace.Emitter.Handler so the ray fires every 0.5 seconds
using a while loop and task.wait, instead of firing on Touched.
When the ray hits Workspace.Target, print "Target spotted!".
Keep the RaycastParams exclusion for the emitter itself.

steps.body:
Press ▶ **Play** (or F5) to run the world. Walk your character onto
the emitter — you should see a line like `Ray hit: Workspace.Target
at ...` print in the Output window each time you step on it.
Shift+F5 to stop.
